pr.title: feat(card): inline card-form widgets + step-by-step UX + optional Banxa partner
pr.body:
## Summary

Direct response to your feedback "_the methods you've added are not working they just tell users to sign up they need easy process and instructions like on the direct send usdt_".

Three changes, all on the card-payment side of `/pay.html`:

### 1. Card form now opens *inline on PoffBank*, not on the provider's signup page
- `ChangeNOW` and `Guardarian` both publish iframe widgets at `/embeds/exchange-widget/v2/widget.html` and `/calculator/v1` respectively. These widgets render **only the card form** â€” no header, no "Log In / Sign Up", no marketing.
- Submitting "Pay with card" now renders the widget in an iframe right below the chooser. The customer types card + does ID verification *without ever leaving PoffBank*.
- Provider's wallet param is still preset to `TURXbzSQQKTiA6fqMzsZMaFQyXAU7o2nXh` (ChangeNOW: `address=`; Guardarian: `payout_address=`).
- I verified neither URL sets `X-Frame-Options` or `frame-ancestors` CSP, so the iframe loads cleanly.
- "Open in new tab â†—" link kept on the iframe header in case the customer's browser blocks iframes (rare, but it's a graceful fallback to the non-embedded URL).

### 2. Step-by-step instructions matching the "Pay direct (USDT)" clarity
Previously: "Pick your preferred processor below." (vague)

Now (numbered list under the card-method notice):
1. **Enter your card** on the secure form below (no signup required).
2. **Quick ID check** â€” a selfie and a photo of your ID, done in the same window.
3. **USDT lands** at PoffBank's TRC-20 wallet automatically. Receipt emailed.

Plus a clear note that if one processor says "not supported", the customer can pick another from the chooser and the wallet destination stays the same.

### 3. Optional Banxa tile (off by default, requires merchant KYB)
Banxa is a B2B aggregator â€” *unlike* ChangeNOW / Guardarian, it does **not** have a public no-KYB endpoint. The merchant has to pass Banxa KYB and gets issued a partner subdomain (e.g. `poffbank.banxa.com`).

- New env var `BANXA_PARTNER_SUBDOMAIN`. When unset, the Banxa tile is completely hidden â€” no behaviour change.
- When set, Banxa becomes the new default provider and `https://{subdomain}.banxa.com/?walletAddress=TURX...` opens in a new tab on submit.
- Reasoning + commitment: Banxa coverage includes Uganda + most of Africa via Mercuryo, so if you ever do get approved as a partner, flipping one env var unlocks it. Until then, ChangeNOW and Guardarian are the day-zero working paths.

### 4. **GoDaddy** (your other question) â€” *not viable*, here's why
- GoDaddy Payments is US-only merchant acquiring for traditional e-commerce. Requires US business, US bank, US tax ID. Ugandan merchants can't sign up.
- Even if you could, it settles in **USD to a US bank account**, not USDT to a TRC-20 wallet. There's no GoDaddy product that does cardâ†’crypto.
- Conclusion: no GoDaddy code added; this PR sticks with the no-KYB aggregators (ChangeNOW + Guardarian) + Banxa partner-flow when you're ready.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

Yellow risk â€” frontend-heavy change, no destructive backend behavior, but iframe widgets are external dependencies whose behavior can change.

- [ ] Open `/pay.html` on the Netlify preview, confirm the numbered step list (`1. Enter your card / 2. Quick ID check / 3. USDT lands`) renders above the provider chooser when "Pay with card" is selected.
- [ ] Click "Pay with card" â†’ ChangeNOW â†’ "Continue to Card Payment". Confirm an **iframe appears inline** showing a fiatâ†’USDT TRC-20 form (not a redirect, not a signup page). The iframe's "Open in new tab â†—" link should still work as a fallback.
- [ ] Repeat for Guardarian â€” same inline iframe behaviour expected.
- [ ] In DevTools â†’ Network, inspect the request to `/api/card-onramp` and confirm the response now includes an `embedUrl` alongside `onrampUrl` for `changenow` and `guardarian`. Banxa / MoonPay should have `onrampUrl` only (no embed).
- [ ] Confirm the Banxa tile is **NOT** in the chooser by default (because `BANXA_PARTNER_SUBDOMAIN` is unset). If you ever get Banxa partner approval, set that env var on Render and the tile will appear automatically.
- [ ] Try the card flow from a Ugandan IP if possible (I cannot do this from the VM). Expected: ChangeNOW widget shows the card form regardless of region; MoonPay will reject Uganda; Guardarian is the strongest Africa fallback via Mercuryo.

### Test plan to verify end-to-end
1. `PORT=4113 node server-clean.js` locally â€” boot banner should show `Card on-ramp: enabled â†’ changenow ..., guardarian ..., moonpay-public ...` and `Default route: changenow`. (Add `BANXA_PARTNER_SUBDOMAIN=poffbank` to see Banxa appear.)
2. `curl -s http://localhost:4113/api/config | jq .card.providers` â€” confirm `changenow` and `guardarian` have `"embeddable": true`.
3. `curl -X POST http://localhost:4113/api/card-onramp -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"amount":50,"provider":"changenow"}'` â€” confirm response has both `onrampUrl` AND `embedUrl`, both containing `wallet=TURXbzSQQKTiA6fqMzsZMaFQyXAU7o2nXh`.
4. Open `http://localhost:4113/pay.html?amount=50` in a browser â†’ Pay with card â†’ Continue â†’ iframe should load the ChangeNOW widget inline.

### Notes
- No backend logic change for existing providers (Transak / MoonPay-partner / NOWPayments-card / MoonPay-public). All keep redirect-only behavior.
- The `embedUrl` field is **only returned** for providers that support iframe widgets (currently `changenow` + `guardarian`). The frontend treats it as opt-in: if `embedUrl` is absent, falls back to redirect. So this PR is strictly additive.
- I considered using ChangeNOW's `/exchange?fiatMode=true` path (a "guest checkout" version of their main site), but the dedicated `/embeds/exchange-widget/` URL is *purpose-built* for iframe embedding (smaller, no marketing chrome, no auth wall at all). That's a better fit for "card form on PoffBank".
- Related: this builds on PR #2 (the multi-provider chooser). The chooser stays; what's new is the inline rendering + step instructions + Banxa scaffolding.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/d4017cddfb9f44ec80fda8c51bf26085
Requested by: @Carlin5
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
